title: Technology
text:
AIData & WebEnablementMediaProductivity


I’ve used AI tools at every level of content generation and learning experience design.
At ZoomInfo, I leveraged our internal “ZI Chat” tool to create and deploy agents that aided the GTM org. Our enablement team set up multiple agents for our own use and I used those tools to plan and design programs related to data products. That output was used inside tools like Articulate Rise to generate skeleton SCORM files that I then edited. I created text, audio, video, images, simulations, podcasts, interactive games, and field-facing product landing pages using AI tools. This content was used to produce high-quality, engaging enablement content for field readiness campaigns.
Additionally, I frequently leveraged our AI tools to query our own data such as using DataDog to examine availability of specific content and then using our Snowflake connector to explore the data itself. I developed internal “micro-apps” to support our international sales team so they could stay fully aware of our data coverage in international markets and to support our own Revenue Enablement team’s measurement and evaluation program to identify impact at scale.



Code: R, Python, SQL, Quarto, Shiny, Processing (Javascript), Manim (Python), HTML, CSS, Markdown, JSON
IDEs: RStudio, Positron, Quarto, Jupyter Notebooks, Colab, VS Code, GitHub
Analysis: Regression, Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), Quantitative Modeling, Factor Analysis, Cluster Analysis




Platform: Canvas (LMS), Seismic (CMS), Allego (LMS)
Content: Canva, H5P, Articulate Rise, Synthesia, Riverside.fm, Google Colab, Notebook LM, Descript, Loom, ElevenLabs




Video & Audio: DaVinci Resolve, OBS Studio, Adobe Creative Suite
Experience Design: UI/UX principles, interaction design, interactive simulations
Production: technical video direction, studio/field recording, and motion graphics




Systems: Jira, Wrike
Productivity: Slack, Teams, Zoom, Confluence, Miro, Excel & Google Suite (Colab, Sheets, Docs, Vids)

title: About
About
text:
Career

Revenue EnablementLearning Experience DesignProgram Management



ZoomInfo
I was the Data Enablement Lead, Revenue Enablement for ZoomInfo’s data and data products supporting a large Go-to-Market (GTM) team of account managers, account executives, customer success & customer experience managers, solutions consultants, and more. I frequently partnered with internal sales and technical teams to develop highly engaging training content such as the award-winning modular DaaS 101 bootcamp that was associated with a 40% uplift in our Data-as-a-Service (DaaS) products.
Notable work products: - DaaS 101 (DaaS Sales Strategy, APIs, Data Cubes) - Leveraging Data Services Intelligence; DevPortal - Introduction to RingLead; GTM Intelligence Platform - GTM Studio 201 - ZoomInfo Data: Origins, Processes, and Value - ZoomInfo Data: Objection Handling in Data Solution Sales




MIT
I consulted for MIT to create, develop, and deliver data science content for the Program in Data Science offered through the Universidad Tecnólogica (UTEC, Uruguay) which leveraged the MITx MicroMasters Program in Statistics and Data Science. Courses: 6.431x “Probability - The Science of Uncertainty and Data” and 14.310x “Data Analysis for Social Scientists”
I created the creative lesson “Didgeridata” blending world music, hands-on invention, and climate data studies for the MIT pK-12 STEAM Camp, an international K-12 education program hosted by the Chinese International School in Hong Kong. This lesson was internally adopted by MIT for the Full STEAM Ahead program. This program was deployed (by me) in New York City and then later by MIT colleagues in China and at MIT for downstream Full Steam Ahead workshops.


Carnegie Mellon University
Led the instructional design for the AI for Business course which was a joint production of the Tepper School of Business and the Department of Computer Science Executive Education program. Led a small team of instructional designers and collaborated with 30+ faculty and several administrators to establish the foundational design for a four-pillar, 16-module program. Collaborated with media personnel to ensure content was recorded and delivered to a partnering production contractor.


Emeritus.org
I worked as a Data Instructional Designer for Emeritus for several years and later as an instructor for two courses. I also contributed to a modular professional certificate as faculty for a data visualization track.

Ziplines Education
I worked as a consultant on several courses: Artificial Intelligence, Large Language Models, Prompt Engineering, SQL




FSU Learning Systems Institute
I was a key team member at Florida State University’s Learning System Institute where I led and managed projects and programs including:

CPALMS Perspectives: a collection of 400+ short-format documentary videos featuring experts, educators, and practitioners speaking about math and science topics. Collaborated with an internal video production team to bring this to life from scratch. Developed a statewide network of contributing subject matter experts, coordinated logistics, established the content vision for the program, and produced training content to enable teachers and school district personnel to leverage this content for teacher professional development and classroom use.
3D printing: Pioneered 3D printing for K-12 classroom use. In collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, developed 50+ models and associated curriculum along with professional development training programs. Our work eventually spun out as mystemkits.com.
Diversity & Ecology Workshops: I led two summer programs for statewide teacher professional development focused on ecological methodology. I coordinated a small team including a university professor, a field biologist, and several classroom teachers. Extended existing training content to include a focus on data science in the classroom. Introduced teachers to open-source data analysis tools and provided guided instruction for end-to-end field science projects.

title: About
text:
Education

PhDMSMSBSCertificates


I completed my PhD in Technology at Purdue University where I completed research on video game players and spatial ability. I developed a novel taxonomy for characterizing video games based on visual-spatial features and then had several hundred respondents describe their most-played games and complete several spatial ability tests. I used machine learning analysis techniques to group respondents by game type and then analyzed their spatial ability performance by group and gender.


I completed my second Master’s degree in Technology at Purdue University where I researched interactive computer graphics, virtual reality, and learning in dynamic virtual environments. I created a novel VR environment and explored how learning could take place withing such an environment.


I completed my first Master’s degree in Horticulture at Purdue University where I studied the genetic response of plants to touch. My model system was soybeans that I grew in a dark room to isolate non-photosynthetic development.


I completed my Bachelor’s degree in Biology at Henderson State University and completed independent genetics research on aquatic invertebrates.

title: About
text:
Volunteering & Mentoring

MIT ReACTBreak Through Tech AI ProgramNASA


Mentor for learners in the Certificate in Computer and Data Science program which includes unemployed or underemployed individuals from refugee, migrant and low income communities located in over 30 countries. MIT Emerging Talent develops global education programs that target the needs of talented individuals from the most challenging economic and social circumstances, by equipping them with the knowledge and tools to advance their education and careers.


Mentor for Cornell University’s Break Through Tech AI Program which provides any undergraduate student (with a focus on Black, Latino/a, Indigenous, and low-income women and non-binary students) with industry focused A.I./M.L. skills training, industry mentors, professional readiness training and opportunities to work on real-world A.I./M.L. projects for major employers and research institutions.


Served as one of three Coordinators for the NASA Spaceflight & Life Sciences Training Program administred by Tuskeegee University. Worked as a liaison between NASA life scientists, program administration, and undergraduate participants to develop their research and educational experience within the program. Delivered instruction and mentoring to student participants for the duration of the six-week program.

title: About
text:
Public Speaking

ConferencesWorkshopsPanelsEngagementList


Frequent presenter at major industry events such as NSTA, FETC, and the NSF Annual Conference, focusing on the intersection of data visualization, 3D technology, and educational standards.


Led intensive workshops for Citizen Schools (Boston) and Lemelson-MIT (Cambridge), training educators on inventive mindsets and technical skill-building.


Served as a subject matter expert on panels (e.g., FSU Stacking Layers Symposium) and presented peer-reviewed research for organizations like the American Society for Gravitational & Space Biology.


Experienced in delivering high-impact virtual sessions for organizations like Carolina Biological, reaching global audiences through digital platforms.

title: Portfolio

Analysis

Data ScienceScienceProduct CharacterizationDidgeridata


These are modules I created for the MIT xPRO course (14.310x) called “Data Analysis for Social Scientists”.

Module 01: Part 1: Bar Charts & Leaflet Maps
Module 01: Part 2: Scatterplots
Module 02: Fundamentals
Module 03: Histograms, Kernel Density Estimates, & Joint Density Plots
Module 04: Markov Inequality Simulation
Module 05: Distributions and qnorm()
Module 06: Maximum (Log) Likelihood Estimation
Module 07: Nonparametric Regression
Module 08: Linear Regression
Module 09: Fuzzy Regression Discontinuity Design
Module 10: Design Principles of Data Visualization
Module 11: Random Forest Regression & Sentiment Analysis
Module 12: Review: Sharp Regression Discontinuity & Nonparametric Regression



These instructional analyses were created for a teacher professional development program at the Learning Systems Institute at Florida State University. The goal was to provide concrete, practical examples of data analysis for science education to help teachers learn how to implement scientific analyses in the classroom using open-source programming tools that were also used by practicing scientists.


This Fountain Pen Ink Analysis represents a quantitative analysis of over 500 fountain pen inks using over 15,000 user-provided reviews from The Goulet Pen Company.


Didgeridata is a multidisciplinary outreach experience that was created for MIT to fuse music, engineering, data exploration, and storytelling.
